items.title: Strait talk on Hormuz: Insults, chaos, shadow of Iraq, keep Trump's allies at bay?
items.description: Day 17 of the war in the Middle East and as the missiles and the drones keep falling, the US President has done a U turn on needing help from his friends and allies. Trump is calling now for a coalition to keep open the Strait of Hormuz. The US strikes on Iran's Kharg Island on Friday 13th sent a message to Tehran, but it did not take away the potential threat to shipping in and out of the Persian Gulf. For Trump, this call to stand together is something of a turn-around. On March 7th, Trump told US network CBS it's a bit late of the United Kingdom and others to send ships. He didn't need British help to win his war. Now, Trump is calling on NATO allies to join him in the war he started on February 28th without consulting them. There's a common interest in keeping the Strait of Hormuz open. But would it even be at risk if Trump had not started the whole crisis?

items.title: US allies 'know how to deal with Trump' amidst threats over Strait of Hormuz

He threatened that refusal to help would mean a 'very bad' future for NATO. But US allies refuse to be dragged into the ongoing war in the Middle East. FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more.

items.title: Ghassan Salamé urges Israel and international community to let Lebanon 'do Its job more efficiently'
items.description: Amid Israel's further military escalation, and Hezbollah's "illegal armed activities", Lebanon is confronted with an acute humanitarian and political crisis. To better understand the impact the conflict is having all across Lebanese society, FRANCE 24's Mark Owen is pleased to welcome Ghassan Salamé, Lebanon's Minister of Culture. According to Mr. Salamé, Lebanon’s sovereignty and stability will not be restored through external intervention but through strengthening the capacity of the Lebanese state itself.

items.title: Moving English tests for migrants online risks criminal abuse, providers warn

items.title: France seeing a 're-polarization and reorganization' of the two poles, left and right
items.description: Genie Godula is pleased to welcome Paul Smith, Head of Modern Languages at the University of Nottingham. In his view, one of the most significant developments is the continued use of proportional list systems across communes of all sizes, from Paris to the smallest rural municipalities. What is politically more revealing, however, is the strategic manoeuvring between the two rounds of voting. In some municipalities, discussions between left-wing forces are underway, while in others, particularly large cities such as Paris and Marseille, candidates appear more inclined to forgo alliances.

items.title: Chile’s new president launches border barrier plan to curb illegal immigration
items.description: Chile’s new far-right president, José Antonio Kast, began preparations on Monday to build a fortified barrier along the country’s northern frontier as part of a sweeping crackdown on illegal immigration. The move, announced less than a week after he took office, reflects a tougher approach to border security and rising public concern over crime.

items.description: The elections on Sunday are widely expected to ‌extend incumbent President Denis Sassou Nguesso's decades-long rule.

items.title: Scores of Pro-Iran Shias defy ban to rally in Nigeria's capital
items.description: Scores of pro-Iran Shia Muslims gathered at the Abuja National Mosque on Friday, defying a heavy security presence to mark Quds Day with anti-Israel and anti-US chants.

items.title: Millions without electricity as Cuba's power grid collapses
items.description: Cuba's chronic fuel shortages have been exacerbated by a US blockade on oil shipments to the island.

items.title: $LIBRA scandal is Milei’s greatest threat
items.description: Revelations about an alleged US$5 million agreement for the ‘$LIBRA’ memecoin strengthen bribery hypothesis surrounding crypto scandal. At a time when confidence indicators in Milei's administration are falling, the scandal can only escalate. Leer más

items.title: Azerbaijan: Frenchman sentenced to 10 years for spying
items.description: French citizen Martin Ryan pled guilty to some of the accusations that he was collecting intelligence on behalf of Paris.

items.title: Utah bereavement author found guilty of fatally poisoning her husband
items.description: A jury found that Kouri Richins killed her husband in March 2022 by poisoning him with a fentanyl-laced drink.

items.title: José Antonio Kast, Chile's most right-wing president since Pinochet, takes office
items.description: José Antonio Kast's rise comes amid a conservative wave sweeping Latin America and after Trump's re-election in the United States. Leer más

items.title: UN receives reports alleging torture of detainees in Venezuela continues
items.description: The UN is deeply concerned by allegations there have been incidents of torture after Nicolás Maduro's ouster.

items.title: Practical ways to make your tank of fuel last longer
items.description: From fuel-efficient driving to alternative travel modes, we look at practical ways to combat fuel shortages and price hikes.

items.title: China overlooks Trump's demands for Hormuz strait, calls for all sides to de-escalate
items.description: China on Monday called on all sides to halt military operations in the Strait of Hormuz to avoid further escalation. The remarks came after US President Donald Trump urged China and six other countries to send warships to secure the vital shipping route. Beijing however made no mention of military intervention. Its incentive to meet Trump's demands appears limited. FRANCE 24's August Hakansson reports.

items.title: Iran's underground arsenal, decentralised military key to press on with strikes on Gulf states
items.description: Reporting from Doha in Qatar for FRANCE 24, Hoda Abdel-Hamid explains that Iran has been able to keep striking Gulf countries, despite claims made by US President Donald Trump that its arsenal had been decimated, because most of its weapons may be underground and the country has decentralised its military, giving regional commanders the right to call the shots.

items.title: Celebrated philosopher Jürgen Habermas dies aged 96
items.description: One of Germany's greatest postwar intellectuals, he continuously argued for a robust and inclusive democracy. His passing marks the end of an era.

items.title: ‘Sleeping in our car is the only option’: Displaced Lebanese face skyrocketing rents
items.description: Hezbollah’s entry into the war against Israel on March 2, in retaliation for the US-Israeli strikes on its Iranian ally, has triggered a massive displacement of civilians across Lebanon. With many shelters at capacity and rental prices skyrocketing, many now find themselves sleeping in the streets or in their cars.

items.title: Watch: Allies reluctant as Trump urges ships to secure Strait of Hormuz
items.description: European countries have resisted U.S. President Donald Trump’s demand for help in clearing the Strait of Hormuz, showing reluctance to get dragged into the escalating U.S.-Israeli war on Iran

items.title: Canada, France, Germany, U.K. warn against major Israeli ground offensive in Lebanon
items.description: The statement also said the countries ‌support efforts by ​the Lebanese government to disarm Iran-backed ⁠militant group Hezbollah

items.title: US becoming dominant military force in Northern Territory, report says
items.description: Defence analysts have criticised planning around the Northern Territory's defence infrastructure, saying Australia risks becoming the secondary user of the NT's geographic advantages, behind the US military.

items.title: News24 | Trump vows ‘absolute right’ to charge tariffs, despite US Supreme Court ruling
items.description: US President Donald Trump said he had the authority to impose duties despite the US Supreme Court striking down his global tariffs.

items.title: African economic growth threatened as oil prices surge
items.description: An expert says most African countries have fuel reserves for just 15 to 25 days, compared to the International Energy Agency standard of 90 days.

items.title: Meta and TikTok let harmful content rise after evidence outrage drove engagement, say whistleblowers
items.description: Companies allowed more harmful content on user’s feeds, knowing their algorithms ran on outrage, BBC hears.

items.title: Kenya says Russia will stop recruiting its citizens to fight in Ukraine
items.description: Kenya says Russia has agreed to stop accepting Kenyan nationals into its military after concerns that hundreds of citizens had been drawn into the war in Ukraine.

items.title: South African soldiers dismantle illegal mining activities near Johannesburg
items.description: Police officers and the army targeted illegal mining sites in the Gauteng Province, according to authorities.

items.title: Watch: ‘We don’t know if he’s dead or not’: Trump on Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei
Belarus' Kolesnikova receives Charlemagne Prize in Germany
items.description: Belarusian opposition figure Maria Kolesnikova was awarded the Aachen-based prize in 2022 in absentia. She has now collected it after having been released from prison in December.

items.title: Trump accuses Iran of using AI to spread disinformation
items.description: Between the TruthSocial post and his comments to journalists, Mr. Trump on March 15 cited three instances where ‌he claimed Iran used AI to mislead the public

items.title: Forensic team identifies 12 new dictatorship victims at Córdoba’s ‘La Perla’ site
items.description: Court in Córdoba confirms skeletal remains found at La Perla detention centre last year have been identified and pertain to 12 missing dictatorship victims; Former detention centre operated in the La Calera area, on the outskirts of the provincial capital. Leer más

items.title: Vietnam firms vie for crypto licences as Hanoi plans ban on overseas trading
Track Cyclone Narelle live as it approaches Queensland coast
items.description: Track the path of Tropical Cyclone Narelle as the system approaches the far north Queensland coast.

items.title: Why did only two Iranian football players stay in Australia?
items.description: After Australia gave humanitarian visas to seven team members, only two decided to stay - the BBC's Katy Watson explains what happened.

items.title: French mayoral elections: Fractured vote reshapes alliances ahead of runoff
items.description: France’s first round of mayoral elections has exposed a deeply fragmented political landscape, with strong performances from both the hard-left La France Insoumise (LFI) and the far-right National Rally (RN) complicating traditional alliances. As candidates gear up for next Sunday’s runoffs, negotiations, withdrawals and refusals to team up are reshaping the electoral map across the country.

items.title: AI fakes about Iran-U.S. war swirl on X despite policy crackdown
items.description: Even if X's demonetisation policy were strictly enforced, a vast number of X users peddling AI content are not part of the revenue sharing programme, researchers say

items.title: Hungary's rival parties hold parallel rallies ahead of vote
items.description: Viktor Orban is facing his first serious challenge in years in Peter Magyar. The two politicians held rallies on Hungary's national day that outlined very different visions for the country's future.

items.title: Karam Hassan: from Sudanese refugee to l'Oréal executive
items.description: Karam Hassan was arrested the moment he returned home. Sudan, December 2013: fresh from his studies in India, he was accused of opposing the government, imprisoned, then forced to inform on his own neighbors. "It became unbearable, unlivable," he says. "I decided to flee."

items.title: Germany news: Merz faces new election test in Hesse
items.description: The far-right Alternative for Germany party is poised for fresh gains in municipal elections in the central state of Hesse. As fuel prices soar, German MPs are urging measures to offset the pain.

items.title: Milei reform drive on hold as Congress awaits new bills
items.description: La Libertad Avanza delays new legislative push after extraordinary sessions; Political reform, judicial overhaul and tax changes yet to be sent to Congress. Leer más

items.title: Trump administration cannot implement 'sweeping' funding freeze, U.S. court rules
items.description: The freeze ‌implicated up to $3 trillion in federal funding

items.title: News24 | Gunman dead after attack on Michigan synagogue
items.description: An unidentified shooter was killed Thursday after exchanging gunfire with security in an attack on a synagogue on the outskirts of Detroit, Michigan, police said.

items.title: Rashid Khan, Afghanistan cricketers condemn deadly Kabul air strike by Pakistan
items.description: The all-rounder wrote that targeting civilian homes, educational facilities or medical infrastructure, whether intentional or accidental, amounts to a war crime

items.title: News24 | Imprisoned former Brazil leader Bolsonaro taken to hospital: son
items.description: Former Brazilian president Jair Bolsonaro was taken to hospital on Friday after feeling unwell at the prison where he is serving a sentence for plotting a coup, his son said.

items.title: France: Far-right strength put to test in local elections
items.description: Voters in France are heading to the polls in local elections seen as a litmus test for next year's presidential vote. A main focus will be the mayoral contest in Paris.

items.title: Iran war: Why gold prices are not soaring
items.description: Gold is typically seen as a safe haven, especially in times of war and other crises. Notwithstanding the ongoing war in Iran, the price is steady. What's driving the stability?

items.title: Chile’s new President José Antonio Kast to signal swift alignment with Trump
items.description: Kast’s new administration plans to sign broad agreements with the United States to boost cooperation on critical minerals and security matters. Leer más

items.title: Iran war: Trump laments lack of support from allies to secure Hormuz
items.description: Donald Trump called on countries to get involved "quickly" after allies, including Germany, rebuffed his demands to help secure the Strait of Hormuz. DW has the latest.

items.title: Israeli strikes hit southern Lebanese town
items.description: Smoke billowed across southern Lebanon on Monday as the Israeli army said it conducted ground operations in "new locations", deploying troops to target Hezbollah positions as it intensified its campaign against the Iran-backed militant group.

items.title: Northern Nigeria: after years of calm, jihadists attacked Maiduguri city
items.description: Suspected jihadists launched an overnight attack on a Nigerian military post on the outskirts of Maiduguri, a key northeastern city that had not seen such violence in years, police, emergency services and residents said Monday.

items.title: Iran blocks vital oil route as Israel expands its ground operations in Lebanon
items.description: As the war in Iran enters its third week, there's no end in sight. Iran has blocked the Strait of Hormuz, a vital oil route, as Trump urges allies to act and Israel expands its ground operations.

items.title: Entrepenuer's phone records cast doubt on President Milei’s '$LIBRA' defence
items.description: Mobile phone records show President Javier Milei spoke repeatedly with a crypto entrepreneur linked to the token’s launch shortly before promoting it online; Alongside flurry of calls, investigators find draft mobile phone note that refers to possible US$5-million contact. Leer más

items.title: US-Israeli strikes damage Iran's cultural heritage sites
items.description: UNESCO World Heritage Sites are at risk, with several monuments, including palaces and a historic mosque, already damaged by US and Israeli strikes, which began on February 28.

items.title: Zelenskyy meets Macron as Iran war overshadows Ukraine
items.description: The Ukrainian president is in Paris for talks with one of Kyiv's staunchest allies. The meeting comes as the US-Israeli war on Iran has removed pressure on Moscow, and derailed Ukraine peace talks.

items.title: US eases oil sanctions at an ideal time for Russia
items.description: The United States has further eased sanctions on oil from Russia in an attempt to bring energy prices down as strikes on Iran continue. The news is a huge boost to the Kremlin, just when it needed one.

items.title: Iran taking steps to prevent anti-establishment protests, Tehran residents tell BBC
items.description: Residents describe new security checkpoints around the capital, where they say people are stopped and searched.
